skill.name: flow-receiving-review
skill.description: Guides agents through verifying code review feedback before accepting, changing, or pushing back.
skill.summary: Verify code review feedback with evidence before making changes.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, path traversal, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown prose and examples. No executable script, network access, credential handling, or prompt-injection attempt was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low risk from broad procedural instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick blocks as Ruby or shell execution. Lines 10-12, 25-36, 40-58, 62-80, 105-143 are Markdown examples and response templates, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked lines are fenced Markdown text with no script file or execution path. The content is instructional prose and templates.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Lines 147-149 contain Markdown cross-reference links to nearby repository documents. They do not read files, write files, accept user input, or execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only traversal-like text is in Markdown links. No code consumes these paths, so there is no traversal vulnerability.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at line 3. Line 3 is the skill description and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive computation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of line 3 shows only descriptive metadata. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak cryptography use.

content.user_title: Review Code Feedback Before Applying Changes
content.value_statement: Code review comments can be unclear, subjective, or technically wrong. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify feedback, respond clearly, and apply only justified changes.
actual_capabilities: Classifies review comments as bugs, style preferences, performance issues, or security concerns.
Guides verification before implementation by checking evidence, reproduction steps, and project standards.
Provides response templates for agreement, clarification, respectful disagreement, and evidence requests.
Helps avoid blind acceptance of review feedback that could introduce regressions.
Supports integration with a review workflow that tracks changes in an execution log.
limitations: Does not perform automated code analysis or run tests by itself.
Depends on the agent having access to the reviewed code and reviewer comments.
Cannot determine team policy when project standards are missing or ambiguous.
Does not replace human judgment for high-impact architecture or security decisions.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Feedback Triage
use_cases.description: Review each comment, separate required fixes from preferences, and decide what evidence is needed before changing code.

use_cases.title: Agentic Coding Review Response
use_cases.description: Help an AI coding agent respond to review comments without blindly applying changes that may be wrong.

use_cases.title: Reviewer Clarification Workflow
use_cases.description: Create clear follow-up questions when feedback lacks reproduction steps, expected behavior, or technical rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Review Comments
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage of a small review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-receiving-review to classify these review comments. For each one, say whether it is a bug, preference, performance issue, security concern, or unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Before Changing
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate whether feedback should be implemented.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-receiving-review to verify this feedback before making changes. Check the evidence, project standards, possible regressions, and what must be tested.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Review Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-receiving-review to draft responses for these comments. Include agreement, clarification questions, or respectful disagreement based on the evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Complex Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-receiving-review to resolve these conflicting review comments. Identify trade-offs, propose the safest implementation path, and list verification steps before editing.

output_examples.input: A reviewer says to remove validation because it looks redundant.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag this as feedback that needs verification. It would ask whether another layer guarantees the same validation and what tests prove removal is safe.

output_examples.input: A reviewer reports a bug but gives no reproduction steps.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend asking for expected behavior, actual behavior, environment details, and steps to reproduce before guessing the fix.

output_examples.input: A reviewer suggests a style change that conflicts with existing project patterns.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend citing the existing pattern, explaining the trade-off, and asking whether the team wants to change the broader convention.

best_practices: Read every review comment completely before deciding whether to implement it.
Verify feedback with tests, reproduction steps, or project standards before changing code.
Explain disagreements with evidence and propose a concrete alternative.
anti_patterns: Do not implement feedback that you do not understand.
Do not treat every reviewer suggestion as a required code change.
Do not reject feedback without explaining the technical reason.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically fix review comments?
faq.answer: No. It guides review triage and response. The agent still needs to make and verify code changes separately.

faq.question: Can it help when a reviewer is wrong?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages evidence-based pushback with clear reasoning and respectful language.

faq.question: Can it handle security review feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It can classify security concerns and prompt verification, but specialist review may still be needed.

faq.question: Does it require a specific repository structure?
faq.answer: No. It is a process guide, although it references related workflow files from its source repository.

faq.question: Is it useful for small pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps keep even small changes evidence-based and avoids unnecessary edits.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
